texto: Passion is a far better prioritizer than any organization system. Soul refreshment comes from SEEING glory – not getting stuff done.

texto: The Puritans' sense of priorities in life was one of their greatest strengths. Putting God first and valuing everything else in relation to God was a recurrent Puritan theme.
explicacao: Citação de Leland Ryken sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “The Puritans' sense of priorities in life was one of their greatest strengths. texto: It is untrue that fiction is nonutilitarian. The uses of fiction are synonymous with the uses of literature. They include refreshment, clarification of life, self-awareness, expansion of our range of experiences, and enlargement of our sense of understanding and discovery, perception, intensification, expression, beauty , and understanding. Like literature generally, fiction is a form of discovery, perception, intensification, expression, beauty, and understanding. If it is all these things, the question of whether it is a legitimate use of time should not even arise.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Leland Ryken: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “It is untrue that fiction is nonutilitarian. The uses of fiction are synonymous with the uses of …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, books, classics pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de classics e fiction.

texto: It is true that the Puritans banned all recreation on Sundays and all games of chance, gambling, bear baiting, horse racing, and bowling in or around taverns at all times. They did so, not because they were opposed to fun, but because they judged these activities to be inherently harmful or immoral.

texto: Earlier in this century someone claimed that we work at our play and play at our work. Today the confusion has deepened: we worship our work, work at our play, and play in our worship.
